sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) Lev 11:30, an unclean animal, mentioned in connexion with other kinds of lizards; according to Bochart (Hieroz. t. 1, p. 1083), the chameleon (from the root תָּנַשׁ to breathe), from its having been supposed by the ancients to live wholly by inhaling air (Plin. viii. 33). LXX., Vulg. talpa. Saad. lizard.

(2) Lev 11:18 Deu 14:16, an unclean, aquatic bird, LXX. πορφυρίων, i.e. ardea purpurea. Vulg. cygnus.
